# terrible_community_aws_batch_compute_environment (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `compute_environment_name` (String) The name for your compute environment. Up to 128 letters (uppercase and lowercase), numbers, and underscores are allowed.
- `compute_resource_type` (String) The type of compute resource.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `instance_role` (String) The Amazon ECS instance role applied to Amazon EC2 instances in a compute environment.
- `instance_types` (String) The instance types that may be launched.
- `maxv_cpus` (Number) The maximum number of EC2 vCPUs that an environment can reach.
- `minv_cpus` (Number) The minimum number of EC2 vCPUs that an environment should maintain.
- `security_group_ids` (String) The EC2 security groups that are associated with instances launched in the compute environment.
- `service_role` (String) The full Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that allows AWS Batch to make calls to other AWS services on your behalf.
- `subnets` (String) The VPC subnets into which the compute resources are launched.
- `type` (String) The type of the compute environment.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `bid_percentage` (Number) The minimum percentage that a Spot Instance price must be when compared with the On-Demand price for that instance type before instances are launched. For example, if your bid percentage is 20%, then the Spot price must be below 20% of the current On-Demand price for that EC2 instance.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `compute_environment_state` (String) The state of the compute environment. If the state is `ENABLED`, then the compute environment accepts jobs from a queue and can scale out automatically based on queues.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `desiredv_cpus` (Number) The desired number of EC2 vCPUS in the compute environment.
- `ec2_key_pair` (String) The EC2 key pair that is used for instances launched in the compute environment.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `image_id` (String) The Amazon Machine Image (AMI) ID used for instances launched in the compute environment.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `spot_iam_fleet_role` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon EC2 Spot Fleet IAM role applied to a SPOT compute environment.
- `state` (String) Describes the desired state.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `output` (String) returns what action was taken, whether something was changed, invocation and response
